title: Computexchange wire contract
description: Canonical JSON Schema for the messages shared between the Go control plane (control/types.go) and the Rust supplier agent (agent/src/types.rs). This file is the single source of truth for the wire shape (the project 'horizon'): snake_case property names, snake_case string enums, an internally-tagged JobType. It is documentation and validation; the two binaries are checked AGAINST it, they do not generate from it. Keep all three in lockstep.

base64.description: Standard base64 (e.g. the honeypot expected answer).

hw_class.description: Hardware capability class. apple_silicon_cluster (Plane B) is a co-located Mac cluster registering as one worker with SUMMED member memory. The nvidia_* classes are the NVIDIA/CUDA lane, tiered by VRAM (the gating resource on NVIDIA); they are a DISTINCT family from Apple so within-class verification never compares across architectures (FP kernels differ Metal/CUDA).

ref.description: GGUF key | HuggingFace repo id | MLX model id, per kind.

JobType.description: Internally-tagged job descriptor. The `type` field discriminates; the remaining fields are the variant payload, flattened alongside `type` (serde `#[serde(tag = "type")]`). Wire examples: {"type":"embed","batch_size":64}, {"type":"batch_infer","max_tokens":512,"temperature":0.0}.

binary.description: Opt-in compact float32 output (PLANE_D D5/D15). When true the agent emits a binary embedding artifact (magic header + packed little-endian f32 rows) instead of the JSON vectors array; absent/false keeps JSON (the default). Optional — a peer that omits it decodes to false.

language.description: BCP-47 hint, or null to auto-detect.

rubric.description: Arbitrary evaluation rubric (free-form object; structure defined by the buyer).

InputRef.description: One input object: a fetch URL plus its size.

url.description: Presigned S3 GET URL, or inline reference for small payloads.

OutputRef.description: Where the merged result is written.

JobConstraints.description: Narrows which workers may run a job.

data_residency.description: ISO country codes to restrict to, e.g. ["CA","US"]; null = unrestricted.

VerificationPolicy.description: Honeypot/redundancy rates and payout hold for a job.

redundancy_frac.description: Fraction of tasks re-run on the same HW class, e.g. 0.10.

honeypot_frac.description: Fraction of tasks that are known-answer honeypots, e.g. 0.02.

JobManifest.description: The full job description submitted by a buyer and carried in each TaskDispatch.

params.description: Type-specific free-form params (temp, quant, etc.); any JSON value.

BenchResult.description: One benchmark line for a (model, job_type) pair.

job_type.description: The JobType tag string (embed | batch_infer | audio_transcribe | image_gen | eval | lora_finetune).

WorkerCapability.description: What a worker advertises to the control plane on registration.

supported_jobs.description: JobType tag strings this worker can run.

TaskDispatch.description: A task handed to a worker in response to a poll.

result_key.description: Bare S3 object key the worker's result lands at (the key behind output_url's presign). Optional: when present the worker echoes it back as TaskCommit.result_key; when absent the worker derives the key from output_url.

TaskCommit.description: A worker's result submission after a task completes.

result_key.description: S3 object key of the uploaded result.

Heartbeat.description: Periodic liveness + telemetry signal (~every 30s). The resource-protection fields (available/effective memory, reserved headroom, throttled) are the supplier-throttling delta the control plane's safe-dispatch filter reads; they are optional so an older agent still validates. loaded_models is the warm-routing delta (PLANE_D §9 D3): the model ids currently warm in the agent's pool — the control plane upserts a worker_model_state row per id and the scheduler gives a small re-rank bonus to a worker that has the job's model warm (it never overrides the claim's hard filter). Optional so an older agent still validates; the agent reports real warm ids only.

current_task.description: UUID of the task in progress, or null when idle.

effective_memory_gb.description: Allocatable for jobs = available − reserved headroom, GB.

reserved_headroom_gb.description: Memory the operator reserves for their own use, GB.

throttled.description: True while the agent is pausing new claims for memory pressure.

loaded_models.description: Canonical ids of models currently warm in the agent's pool (warm-routing re-rank). Empty when nothing is loaded; real ids only.

JobSubmitRequest.description: The buyer-facing job-submit body for POST /v1/jobs. This is the ergonomic surface a buyer SDK builds; the control plane expands it into the full internal JobManifest (assigning the job id, splitting tasks, presigning input/output). `input` is either an inline reference string (small payloads) or an object pointing at an already-uploaded S3 key.

params.description: Type-specific free-form params (temperature, quant, language, ...); any JSON value.

input.description: Job input: either an inline reference string (small payloads, e.g. a presigned GET URL or inline JSONL marker) or a pointer to a pre-uploaded object by S3 key.

s3_key.description: Object key in the jobs bucket.

webhook_url.description: Optional https URL to POST on job completion (registered into the webhooks table).

max_usd.description: Optional buyer hard spend cap (Budget Governor, Plane C §12 / Plane D §14 D8). When > 0 the scheduler refuses to dispatch a new task once the job's projected charge (already-charged + one task's estimate) would breach it; the task stays queued and budget_state flips to paused_for_budget. Omitted or 0 means no cap.

EmbedResult.description: Result document a worker writes for an embed task (read back by the control plane / buyer). One vector per input line, row-aligned with the input order.

vectors.description: Embedding vectors, each a fixed-length array of dim floats.

BatchInferResult.description: Result document a worker writes for a batch_infer task. One completion per input prompt, row-aligned by index.

tokens.description: Output tokens generated for this completion.

AudioTranscribeResult.description: Result document a worker writes for an audio_transcribe task: the full transcript plus per-segment timestamps.
